mission.title: Install and Configure Dify
mission.description: Dify is a production-ready platform for developing agentic workflows, enabling users to create and manage AI-driven applications. It is ideal for teams looking to leverage AI capabilities in their workflows while maintaining control over their infrastructure. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and resource management.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Dify Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Dify using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add dify https://charts.dify.ai

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Dify Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for Dify to function properly as they define the custom resources used by the application.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs for Dify.

steps.title: Install Dify using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Dify into your Kubernetes cluster using the specified namespace and version.

```bash

helm install dify dify/dify --namespace dify --create-namespace --version 0.12.0

```

> Replace '0.12.0' with the desired version.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
Creating a custom resource is essential to configure and utilize Dify's capabilities.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

```

> This command creates an example Dify application.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the created custom resource to ensure it is running as expected.

```bash

kubectl get difyapp example-app -o yaml

```

> Check the output for the status of the Dify application.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dify
uninstall.description:
This step will remove Dify from your cluster, including all associated resources. Be cautious as this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall dify --namespace dify

kubectl delete namespace dify

```

> This command uninstalls the Dify release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dify
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade Dify to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get difyapp example-app -o yaml > backup-example-app.yaml

helm upgrade dify dify/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.1

kubectl get difyapp example-app -o yaml

```

> Backup the current state of the Dify application.
> Replace '0.12.1' with the desired version.
> Verify the upgrade by checking the status of the Dify application.
commands: kubectl get difyapp example-app -o yaml > backup-example-app.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'DifyApp not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Dify application does not exist in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Dify application name is correct and exists in the specified namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRDs'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRDs may not be accessible or the URL may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the CRD URL and ensure you have network access to it.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be a conflict with existing resources or incorrect parameters.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm release logs for details and resolve any conflicts.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The YAML configuration may have syntax errors or invalid fields.

**Fix:**
Validate the YAML syntax and ensure all required fields are correctly specified.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new workflow features and improved API stability.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older API versions in favor of the new v1 API.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update any existing configurations to use the new v1 API endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get difyapp example-app -o yaml' showing the status as 'Running' and the application details.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
